subject: AREtomo to WARP
body:
hi all,

I started in WARP for movie alignment then exported the stack "to IMOD".   
However I used AREtomo to process instead of Etomo, using the flag -OUTImod 
2 in order to get the xf file for importing into WARP.  The AREtomo 
reconstruction looks fantastic.   Now I reimport into WARP without issue, 
estimate the CTF, check handedness, then reconstruct tomograms.    It all 
succeeds, but the output tomograms are really poor quality and I can't 
figure out why.   They certainly look similar to AREtomo in that I can see 
some proteins where they should be, but the contrast is really poor.  

Inverting contrast in tomo reconstruction didn't help.   The FFT looks nice 
and has resolution to 6A, clear Thon rings which match well with the 
calculated CTF.  AREtomo did not output a TAsolutions.log file that I Can 
find, which I thought WARP needs.  However WARP did seem to successfully 
import the data using just the XF file.   I tried clicking "dont invert 
tilt angles" during import because perhaps AREtomo is different from IMOD 
(explained in WARP tutorial) but that had no effect.  Hard to tell which 
was better.  I tried reprocessing AREtomo without the flag -FlipVol hoping 
that might fix an issue with the coordinate system but it also had no 
effect.  Overall it seems like the protein is definitely visible in the 
final reconstruction but it just looks really poor contrast.  Maybe 
something is going wrong with the CTF correction or...  maybe it's just 
that the WARP reconstruction looks WAY worse than the AREtomo 
reconstruction.   

Any help appreciated, thanks.  

Jesse
